parameters.text:
=You are a proposal operations expert. You receive the text of an RFP/ToR/ITT document.

Your task: extract the REQUIRED proposal response structure and compliance requirements.

IMPORTANT RULES
- Output MUST be a SINGLE valid JSON object.
- No markdown. No commentary. No extra keys.
- Use null when unknown.
- Use arrays ([]) for lists when empty.
- Be conservative: do not invent facts not present in the document.
- If the document includes multiple instruction areas (e.g., "Instructions to Offerors", "Submission Requirements", "Proposal Format"), prioritize those as sources of truth.

OUTPUT JSON SCHEMA (EXACT)
Return a single JSON object with exactly these keys:

{
  "rfp_id": string |{{ $('Download file').item.json.name }} ,
  "rfp_title": string | null,
  "client_org": string | null,
  "procurement_type": "RFP" | "RFQ" | "ToR" | "ITT" | "EOI" | "Other" | null,
  "submission_deadline": {
    "date": string | null,
    "time_local": string | null,
    "timezone": string | null
  },
  "submission_method": {
    "channel": "Email" | "Portal" | "Physical" | "Other" | null,
    "details": string | null
  },
  "formatting_requirements": {
    "page_limit": number | null,
    "font": string | null,
    "font_size": string | null,
    "file_format": "PDF" | "DOCX" | "Other" | null,
    "language": string | null,
    "other_constraints": string[]
  },
  "required_response_structure": [
    {
      "section_id": string,
      "title": string,
      "is_mandatory": boolean,
      "order": number,
      "required_content": string[],
      "page_or_word_limit": string | null,
      "evaluation_alignment": string[],
      "source_quotes": string[]
    }
  ],
  "compliance_checklist": [
    {
      "req_id": string,
      "requirement": string,
      "type": "Must" | "Should" | "Format" | "Eligibility" | "Submission",
      "where_found": string | null,
      "mapped_section_ids": string[],
      "pass_fail": boolean
    }
  ],
  "evaluation_criteria": [
    {
      "criterion": string,
      "weight": string | null,
      "notes": string | null
    }
  ],
  "appendices_and_forms": [
    {
      "name": string,
      "required": boolean,
      "notes": string | null
    }
  ],
  "open_questions": string[]
}

FIELD EXTRACTION RULES
- rfp_id: reference number, tender number, solicitation ID. Else null.
- rfp_title: use the title on cover page or first occurrences. Else null.
- client_org: issuing organization.
- procurement_type: infer from document language (RFP/RFQ/ToR/ITT/EOI). If unclear, null.
- submission_deadline:
  - If multiple deadlines exist, use the FINAL submission deadline.
  - date format: YYYY-MM-DD if possible; else null.
  - time_local: HH:MM 24h if possible; else null.
  - timezone: if stated (e.g. CET, UTC+1); else null.
- submission_method:
  - channel: Email/Portal/Physical/Other based on instructions.
  - details: email address / portal name / physical address snippet.
- formatting_requirements:
  - page_limit: numeric only, else null.
  - other_constraints: include things like "separate technical and financial", "no pricing in technical", "signed forms", "CV max 2 pages", "use template", etc.
- required_response_structure:
  - Create one entry per REQUIRED section in the order requested.
  - If only a broad structure exists (e.g., Technical / Financial / Annexes), still create entries for those.
  - is_mandatory: true if language indicates required; else true by default unless clearly optional.
  - required_content: bullets of what must be covered in the section.
  - source_quotes: up to 2 short verbatim snippets (<= 25 words each) that justify the section requirement.
- compliance_checklist:
  - Convert all "shall/must/required" items + submission rules into checklist entries.
  - pass_fail: true for Must/Format/Eligibility/Submission; false for Should.
  - where_found: short locator like "Section 3.2 Submission Requirements" or "Page 7" if present.
  - mapped_section_ids: link to required_response_structure section_id(s) where this requirement should be addressed; if unclear, [].
- evaluation_criteria:
  - Extract evaluation/scoring criteria and weights if stated.
- appendices_and_forms:
  - Include required annexes, forms, templates, declarations, pricing sheets.
- open_questions:
  - Add any missing info the proposal team must clarify (e.g., “page limit unclear”, “submission portal not stated”, etc.)

INPUT (verbatim)
DOCUMENT_TEXT:
{{ $json.text }}

FILE_ID: {{ $('Download file').item.json.id }}
FILE_NAME: {{ $('Download file').item.json.name }}

OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS
- Return ONLY the JSON object described above.
- Ensure valid JSON (double quotes, no trailing commas).

parameters.text:
=You are a senior proposal strategist. Create a concise “Tender Insights Pack” to help humans draft quickly and comply.

Use ONLY the provided CANONICAL_TENDER_JSON. Do not invent facts. If info is missing, create a clarification question.

Output MUST be a SINGLE valid JSON object with EXACTLY TWO top-level keys:
1) "insights_block"
2) "section_insights"

SCHEMA:

{
  "insights_block": {
    "executive_takeaways": string[],
    "win_themes": [{ "theme": string, "so_what": string, "proof_needed": string[] }],
    "risks": [{ "risk": string, "impact": string, "mitigation": string, "owner": string }],
    "go_no_go_flags": string[],
    "clarification_questions_global": [{ "question": string, "why": string, "blocks": string[] }],
    "critical_human_contributions": [
      { "area": string, "who_to_ask": string, "questions_to_ask": string[], "artifacts_needed": string[] }
    ]
  },
  "section_insights": {
    "<SECTION_ID_FROM_CANONICAL>": {
      "section_summary": string,
      "what_evaluators_want": string[],
      "human_inputs_needed": string[],
      "questions_for_humans": string[],
      "evidence_to_pull": string[],
      "drafting_outline": string[]
    }
  }
}

SECTION RULES:
- The keys of section_insights MUST EXACTLY match tender.required_response_structure[].section_id (e.g., S1, S2, S3).
- For each section, read its required_content and evaluation_alignment and turn them into:
  - "what_evaluators_want" (max 6 bullets)
  - "drafting_outline" (max 8 bullets; ordered)
  - "questions_for_humans" that request ONLY human-only info (availability, proof points, client relationship, logistics, pricing).
- Keep it short and usable tomorrow.

TENDER CONTEXT TO APPLY:
- Respect formatting constraints (page limit, font size, language, no external links) as “non-negotiables”.
- For Concept/Technical section: must explicitly cover strategy, actors/cooperation, steering with partners, operational plan with milestones, knowledge management/scaling, coordination with GIZ, personnel assignment plan (dates/durations/locations/work steps), and backstopping concept.
- For Personnel section: CV compliance (format + max pages + show role & duration) and clear link of qualifications and soft skills to ToR.
- For Costing: follow quantitative parameters exactly; sustainability/CO2 offset; flexible remuneration requires prior approval; limits are maximums.

SECTION IDS (MUST USE EXACTLY THESE KEYS):
{{ $json.tender.required_response_structure.map(s => s.section_id).join(", ") }}

In section_insights, the object keys MUST be exactly the list above, no other keys.

For each section key, you MUST produce exactly: section_summary, what_evaluators_want, human_inputs_needed, questions_for_humans, evidence_to_pull, drafting_outline.

INPUT:
CANONICAL_TENDER_JSON:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.tender) }}

=You are a proposal coach + compliance QA. Your job is to help a HUMAN write ONE proposal section.

You will receive SECTION_CONTEXT_JSON. Use it to generate ONLY section-specific, actionable guidance.

STRICT OUTPUT RULES:
- Output MUST be valid JSON (no markdown, no code fences, no commentary).
- Do NOT output bullets inside strings.
- For list fields, output JSON arrays of strings.
- questions_for_humans MUST be EXACTLY 5 items (no more, no less).
- Keep each list item short, concrete, and “answerable” by a busy SME.

SECTION_CONTEXT_JSON:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.agent2_input) }}

Return a single JSON object with EXACT keys:

{
  "section_id": "string (must match SECTION_CONTEXT_JSON.section.section_id)",
  "section_summary": "2–4 sentences: what this section must achieve + the top scoring levers + any hard compliance constraints",
  "what_evaluators_want": ["5–8 crisp evaluator expectations tied to required_content/evaluation_alignment"],
  "human_inputs_needed": ["5–8 concrete inputs/artifacts needed from humans to draft (e.g., 'draft milestone schedule with dates', 'partner steering model used previously', etc.)"],
  "questions_for_humans": [
    "exactly 5 questions; each asks for specific info/decisions/evidence the writer needs"
  ],
  "evidence_to_pull": ["5–10 specific evidence sources to reuse (past project references, templates, policies, examples, metrics, CV facts, etc.)"],
  "drafting_outline": ["6–12 headings/subheadings in the order the section should be written, aligned to required_content"]
}

Guidance:
- Use SECTION_CONTEXT_JSON.section.required_content as the backbone.
- Incorporate SECTION_CONTEXT_JSON.section.page_or_word_limit and any formatting requirements.
- If compliance_checklist_for_section exists, reflect it in section_summary + what_evaluators_want.
- Be specific: name deliverables, tables, annexes, and proof points the evaluator will look for.

json.rfp_title: Terms of reference (ToR) for the procurement of services below the EU threshold
json.title: Technical-Methodological Concept
json.required_content:
• Explicit strategy addressing objectives of the services
• Description of cooperation with relevant actors
• Approach to steering measures with project partners
• Contribution to results-based monitoring system
• Description of key processes and operational plan/schedule
• Contribution to knowledge management and scaling-up effects
json.evaluation_alignment: • Technical-methodological concept (1.1 to 1.5 in assessment grid)
json.source_quotes:
• The tenderer is required to show how the objectives defined in Chapter 2 are to be achieved
• The tenderer is required to present and justify the explicit strategy
json.section_summary: This section requires a detailed technical-methodological concept explicitly addressing all objectives, describing cooperation, steering with partners, operational planning including schedules and milestones, monitoring contributions, and knowledge management for scaling.
json.what_evaluators_want:
• Clear and explicit strategy aligned with objectives in Chapter 2.
• Description of cooperation and roles of relevant partners.
• Approach to steering and coordination with project partners.
• Operational plan with key processes and milestones.
• Contribution to results-based monitoring system.
• Knowledge management concept and scaling-up effects.
json.human_inputs_needed:
• Input on cooperation partners and coordination mechanisms.
• Operational milestone dates and schedules.
• Description of knowledge management approaches.
json.questions_for_humans:
• What concrete strategy will be used to achieve objectives?
• What partners will be involved and what will be the cooperation model?
• What are the key milestones and deliverables with timelines?
• How will monitoring and scaling be implemented and documented?
json.evidence_to_pull:
• Past project strategies demonstrating successful objective achievement.
• Relationship records with key partners.
• Examples of operational plans and monitoring systems used.
json.drafting_outline:
• Introduction with explicit strategy referencing Chapter 2 objectives.
• Description of cooperation with relevant project actors.
• Approach to steering and managing partnerships.
• Detailed operational plan including key processes and schedule.
• Contribution to monitoring and evaluation framework.
• Knowledge management and approach to scaling.
• Summary and justification of strategic choices.
• Compliance with page and formatting constraints.
json.executive_takeaways:
• The tender must strictly follow the ToR structure and align the technical concept with weighted assessment criteria.
• Content must not exceed 10 pages excluding CVs; CVs have a 4-page limit each and must follow the specified format.
• No external content such as website links is allowed; all text must be in English with font size 11 or larger for legibility.
• Technical-Methodological Concept requires an explicit strategy covering cooperation, steering, operational planning, monitoring, knowledge management, and scaling.
• Personnel section must demonstrate strong link between CV qualifications, soft skills and the ToR requirements with regional experience.
• Costing must precisely follow quantitative parameters, clearly state lump-sum or against-evidence basis, and include travel and accommodation.
json.win_themes:
• Explicit, well-justified strategy aligned with objectives — Demonstrates clear understanding and capability to achieve project goals, increasing evaluator confidence. (Proof: Detailed strategy addressing Chapter 2 objectives; Rationale for chosen methods and cooperation framework)
• Robust coordination and project management approach — Ensures smooth integration with GIZ and effective project delivery with backstopping. (Proof: Personnel assignment plan detailing roles, timelines, work steps; Explanation of coordination and backstopping concepts)
• Highly qualified, regionally experienced personnel with strong CV compliance — Maximizes scoring on personnel criteria, ensuring capability to deliver quality results. (Proof: CVs adhering to format and page limit; Clear demonstration of relevant qualifications and experience)
• Fully compliant, transparent costing aligned with specified parameters — Avoids disqualification, reflects financial discipline and realism in budgeting. (Proof: Price schedule reflecting days, travel, accommodations; Clear lump-sum or budget-against-evidence statement)
json.risks:
• Non-compliance with page limits and formatting constraints — impact: Content or CVs exceeding limits will be disregarded, lowering score or causing rejection. | mitigation: Strict internal review ensuring adherence to page limits and font size prior to submission. | owner: Bid Manager/Proposal Lead
• Lack of clarity on submission deadline and method — impact: Potential late or misdirected submission risks disqualification. | mitigation: Clarify deadline date/time/timezone and submission channel with GIZ immediately. | owner: Proposal Coordinator
• Inadequate demonstration of personnel qualifications and experience — impact: Lower scoring under personnel criteria, reducing competitiveness. | mitigation: Gather detailed evidence linking CV content to ToR qualifications and soft skills. | owner: Human Resources/Technical Lead
• Costing not strictly aligned with Chapter 5 parameters or missing lump-sum declaration — impact: Tender may be rejected or scored lower for financial non-compliance. | mitigation: Careful price calculation and explicit statement on offer type (lump-sum or against evidence). | owner: Finance/Bid Pricing Team
json.go_no_go_flags:
• Deadline date, time, and timezone not specified—Must clarify before proceeding.
• Submission method and file format unclear—Must confirm submission channel and file type.
• Strict page limit for main content and CVs—If unable to comply, consider no-go.
• Must have personnel able to produce CVs per required format and content—if not achievable, consider no-go.
json.critical_human_contributions:
• Technical-Methodological Strategy Definition (Ask: Technical Lead and Project Manager) — Questions: What explicit strategy best addresses the objectives defined in Chapter 2?; Which partners and cooperation actors should be emphasized?; What operational milestones and schedules are realistic?
• Personnel Qualification Alignment (Ask: HR Manager and Personnel Experts) — Questions: Who are the most qualified personnel available matching ToR roles?; Can CVs be prepared to clearly reflect position, duration, and relevant skills?; What soft skills and regional experience can be highlighted?
• Costing and Pricing Preparation (Ask: Finance Manager and Bid Pricing Team) — Questions: What are the exact parameters from Chapter 5 for costing?; How will travel and accommodation be calculated and included?; Is the offer lump-sum or against evidence and how to document this?

json.rfp_title: Terms of reference (ToR) for the procurement of services below the EU threshold
json.title: Project Management of the Contractor
json.required_content:
• Approach for coordination with the GIZ project
• Explanation of project management requirements as per Chapter 2
• Personnel assignment plan with expert details and allocation of work steps
• Description of the backstopping concept
json.source_quotes:
• The tenderer is required to explain its approach for coordination with the GIZ project
• The tenderer is required to draw up a personnel assignment plan with explanatory notes
json.section_summary: Focus on the contractor’s project management approach, explaining coordination with GIZ, detailed personnel assignment plan with expert roles, timelines, and allocation of work, plus the backstopping and support concept.
json.what_evaluators_want:
• Clear approach for coordination with the GIZ project.
• Explanation of project management tailored to ToR requirements.
• Detailed personnel assignment plan covering roles and work steps.
• Description of backstopping and support mechanisms.
json.human_inputs_needed:
• Information on coordination mechanisms with GIZ.
• Personnel availability and allocation details.
• Backstopping resources and contingency plans.
json.questions_for_humans:
• How will coordination with GIZ be managed practically?
• Who are the assigned personnel and what are their allocated tasks and timelines?
• What is the planned backstopping concept in case of personnel changes or challenges?
json.evidence_to_pull:
• Examples of previous coordination approaches with donors.
• Personnel assignment tables and resource plans.
• Descriptions of backstopping and contingency arrangements.
json.drafting_outline:
• Introduction to project management approach.
• Coordination approach with GIZ including communication flows.
• Personnel assignment plan with expert details and timelines.
• Allocation of work steps to personnel.
• Detailed description of backstopping concept.
• Contingency and risk management in project management.
• Summary of how management approach ensures project success.
json.executive_takeaways:
• The tender must strictly follow the ToR structure and align the technical concept with weighted assessment criteria.
• Content must not exceed 10 pages excluding CVs; CVs have a 4-page limit each and must follow the specified format.
• No external content such as website links is allowed; all text must be in English with font size 11 or larger for legibility.
• Technical-Methodological Concept requires an explicit strategy covering cooperation, steering, operational planning, monitoring, knowledge management, and scaling.
• Personnel section must demonstrate strong link between CV qualifications, soft skills and the ToR requirements with regional experience.
• Costing must precisely follow quantitative parameters, clearly state lump-sum or against-evidence basis, and include travel and accommodation.
json.win_themes:
• Explicit, well-justified strategy aligned with objectives — Demonstrates clear understanding and capability to achieve project goals, increasing evaluator confidence. (Proof: Detailed strategy addressing Chapter 2 objectives; Rationale for chosen methods and cooperation framework)
• Robust coordination and project management approach — Ensures smooth integration with GIZ and effective project delivery with backstopping. (Proof: Personnel assignment plan detailing roles, timelines, work steps; Explanation of coordination and backstopping concepts)
• Highly qualified, regionally experienced personnel with strong CV compliance — Maximizes scoring on personnel criteria, ensuring capability to deliver quality results. (Proof: CVs adhering to format and page limit; Clear demonstration of relevant qualifications and experience)
• Fully compliant, transparent costing aligned with specified parameters — Avoids disqualification, reflects financial discipline and realism in budgeting. (Proof: Price schedule reflecting days, travel, accommodations; Clear lump-sum or budget-against-evidence statement)
json.risks:
• Non-compliance with page limits and formatting constraints — impact: Content or CVs exceeding limits will be disregarded, lowering score or causing rejection. | mitigation: Strict internal review ensuring adherence to page limits and font size prior to submission. | owner: Bid Manager/Proposal Lead
• Lack of clarity on submission deadline and method — impact: Potential late or misdirected submission risks disqualification. | mitigation: Clarify deadline date/time/timezone and submission channel with GIZ immediately. | owner: Proposal Coordinator
• Inadequate demonstration of personnel qualifications and experience — impact: Lower scoring under personnel criteria, reducing competitiveness. | mitigation: Gather detailed evidence linking CV content to ToR qualifications and soft skills. | owner: Human Resources/Technical Lead
• Costing not strictly aligned with Chapter 5 parameters or missing lump-sum declaration — impact: Tender may be rejected or scored lower for financial non-compliance. | mitigation: Careful price calculation and explicit statement on offer type (lump-sum or against evidence). | owner: Finance/Bid Pricing Team
json.go_no_go_flags:
• Deadline date, time, and timezone not specified—Must clarify before proceeding.
• Submission method and file format unclear—Must confirm submission channel and file type.
• Strict page limit for main content and CVs—If unable to comply, consider no-go.
• Must have personnel able to produce CVs per required format and content—if not achievable, consider no-go.
json.critical_human_contributions:
• Technical-Methodological Strategy Definition (Ask: Technical Lead and Project Manager) — Questions: What explicit strategy best addresses the objectives defined in Chapter 2?; Which partners and cooperation actors should be emphasized?; What operational milestones and schedules are realistic?
• Personnel Qualification Alignment (Ask: HR Manager and Personnel Experts) — Questions: Who are the most qualified personnel available matching ToR roles?; Can CVs be prepared to clearly reflect position, duration, and relevant skills?; What soft skills and regional experience can be highlighted?
• Costing and Pricing Preparation (Ask: Finance Manager and Bid Pricing Team) — Questions: What are the exact parameters from Chapter 5 for costing?; How will travel and accommodation be calculated and included?; Is the offer lump-sum or against evidence and how to document this?

json.rfp_title: Terms of reference (ToR) for the procurement of services below the EU threshold
json.title: Costing Requirements and Price Schedule
json.required_content:
• Calculation of financial tender based on specified parameters
• Inclusion of personnel assignment days, travel and accommodation expenses
• Clear note if offer is on lump-sum basis or against evidence
json.source_quotes:
• Please calculate your financial tender based exactly on the parameters specified in Chapter 5
• Please indicate in the price schedule whether your offer is on a lump-sum basis or against evidence
json.section_summary: Costing section must strictly align with financial parameters, including personnel days, travel, and accommodation costs, and clearly declare if the offer is lump-sum or based on evidence.
json.what_evaluators_want:
• Exact financial calculation per Chapter 5 parameters.
• Inclusion of all necessary costs: personnel, travel, accommodation.
• Clear indication if pricing is lump-sum or against evidence.
• Transparent and realistic budgeting compliant with limits.
json.human_inputs_needed:
• Validated cost rates and parameters from Chapter 5.
• Travel and accommodation expense data.
• Decision on pricing approach (lump-sum or evidence-based).
json.questions_for_humans:
• What are the exact parameters for pricing from Chapter 5?
• How will travel and accommodation be calculated and justified?
• Is the offer lump-sum or based on actual evidence?
• Are any flexible remunerations included and have they been approved?
json.evidence_to_pull:
• Official cost parameters and tariffs.
• Previous cost models used for similar projects.
• Price schedule templates and cost calculation sheets.
json.drafting_outline:
• Introduction confirming financial compliance with Chapter 5.
• Detailed cost breakdown for personnel assignment days.
• Travel and accommodation costs included and justified.
• Statement on lump-sum vs. evidence-based offer.
• Any notes on flexible remuneration if applicable.
• Summary ensuring transparency and compliance.
json.executive_takeaways:
• The tender must strictly follow the ToR structure and align the technical concept with weighted assessment criteria.
• Content must not exceed 10 pages excluding CVs; CVs have a 4-page limit each and must follow the specified format.
• No external content such as website links is allowed; all text must be in English with font size 11 or larger for legibility.
• Technical-Methodological Concept requires an explicit strategy covering cooperation, steering, operational planning, monitoring, knowledge management, and scaling.
• Personnel section must demonstrate strong link between CV qualifications, soft skills and the ToR requirements with regional experience.
• Costing must precisely follow quantitative parameters, clearly state lump-sum or against-evidence basis, and include travel and accommodation.
json.win_themes:
• Explicit, well-justified strategy aligned with objectives — Demonstrates clear understanding and capability to achieve project goals, increasing evaluator confidence. (Proof: Detailed strategy addressing Chapter 2 objectives; Rationale for chosen methods and cooperation framework)
• Robust coordination and project management approach — Ensures smooth integration with GIZ and effective project delivery with backstopping. (Proof: Personnel assignment plan detailing roles, timelines, work steps; Explanation of coordination and backstopping concepts)
• Highly qualified, regionally experienced personnel with strong CV compliance — Maximizes scoring on personnel criteria, ensuring capability to deliver quality results. (Proof: CVs adhering to format and page limit; Clear demonstration of relevant qualifications and experience)
• Fully compliant, transparent costing aligned with specified parameters — Avoids disqualification, reflects financial discipline and realism in budgeting. (Proof: Price schedule reflecting days, travel, accommodations; Clear lump-sum or budget-against-evidence statement)
json.risks:
• Non-compliance with page limits and formatting constraints — impact: Content or CVs exceeding limits will be disregarded, lowering score or causing rejection. | mitigation: Strict internal review ensuring adherence to page limits and font size prior to submission. | owner: Bid Manager/Proposal Lead
• Lack of clarity on submission deadline and method — impact: Potential late or misdirected submission risks disqualification. | mitigation: Clarify deadline date/time/timezone and submission channel with GIZ immediately. | owner: Proposal Coordinator
• Inadequate demonstration of personnel qualifications and experience — impact: Lower scoring under personnel criteria, reducing competitiveness. | mitigation: Gather detailed evidence linking CV content to ToR qualifications and soft skills. | owner: Human Resources/Technical Lead
• Costing not strictly aligned with Chapter 5 parameters or missing lump-sum declaration — impact: Tender may be rejected or scored lower for financial non-compliance. | mitigation: Careful price calculation and explicit statement on offer type (lump-sum or against evidence). | owner: Finance/Bid Pricing Team
json.go_no_go_flags:
• Deadline date, time, and timezone not specified—Must clarify before proceeding.
• Submission method and file format unclear—Must confirm submission channel and file type.
• Strict page limit for main content and CVs—If unable to comply, consider no-go.
• Must have personnel able to produce CVs per required format and content—if not achievable, consider no-go.
json.critical_human_contributions:
• Technical-Methodological Strategy Definition (Ask: Technical Lead and Project Manager) — Questions: What explicit strategy best addresses the objectives defined in Chapter 2?; Which partners and cooperation actors should be emphasized?; What operational milestones and schedules are realistic?
• Personnel Qualification Alignment (Ask: HR Manager and Personnel Experts) — Questions: Who are the most qualified personnel available matching ToR roles?; Can CVs be prepared to clearly reflect position, duration, and relevant skills?; What soft skills and regional experience can be highlighted?
• Costing and Pricing Preparation (Ask: Finance Manager and Bid Pricing Team) — Questions: What are the exact parameters from Chapter 5 for costing?; How will travel and accommodation be calculated and included?; Is the offer lump-sum or against evidence and how to document this?
